description_official: DRAFT — verify against current AFECD. Cyber Defense Operations airmen execute defensive cyberspace operations, analyzing cyber threats and responding to incidents on Air Force networks.
description_plain: Defensive cyber for the Air Force. You run intrusion detection, hunt threats on Air Force networks, and respond to incidents. Classroom-heavy pipeline, fast-evolving skill set, and the clearance you earn is a career asset for decades after you separate.
description_tldr: Air Force defensive cyber operator — monitors, hunts, and responds to threats on Air Force networks.

asvab.notes: Air Force uses four aptitude composites: Mechanical (M), Administrative (A), General (G), Electronics (E).

training_pipeline.description: DRAFT — verify current course length and structure.

compensation.typical_entry_rank: E-3 (Airman First Class) on standard contract

salary_range_usd.notes: High-paying civilian field with strong demand; TS/SCI clearance adds a 20-40% premium.
Requires offensive cyber training beyond defensive 1D7X1 baseline.

daily_life: Mostly office / SCIF work. You're watching network telemetry, hunting for anomalies, responding to security events, and writing reports. Rotating shifts are common for 24/7 SOC coverage. Classified work means you're in a SCIF (secure facility) without personal devices. Less physical than most AFSCs — pipeline is classroom-heavy.

faq.question: How long is the tech school?
faq.answer: Keesler cyber tech training runs approximately 6-9 months of block instruction depending on the specific pipeline and current course length. Verify current length in the AFECD.

faq.question: What clearance do I need?
faq.answer: Top Secret (TS) minimum. Many billets require TS/SCI. The background investigation starts during tech school and typically takes 6-18 months; you may be assigned interim access during processing.

faq.question: Can I take DoD COOL certs on active duty?
faq.answer: Yes. DoD COOL funds CompTIA (Security+, CySA+, CASP+), ISACA (CISM), (ISC)² (CISSP), and others. Many airmen stack 3-5 certs during a 4-year term.

faq.question: Does the Air Force let me work remote cyber?
faq.answer: Limited. Classified networks require in-person work at a cleared facility. Unclassified work can occasionally be remote, but the default is on-site.

notable_holders.name: General Timothy D. Haugh
notable_holders.notable_for: Commander of U.S. Cyber Command and Director of NSA (representative cyber leadership role; not necessarily 1D7X1).
notable_holders.notes: Included as an example of Air Force cyber career pinnacle.

promotion_speed.fast_promote_notes: Cert stacking, Community College of the Air Force (CCAF) degree, and strong EPRs accelerate promotion in Air Force. Cyber field has historically had lower E-5/E-6 cutoff scores than oversubscribed AFSCs.
promotion_speed.slow_promote_notes: Air Force promotion uses WAPS (Weighted Airman Promotion System) — heavy emphasis on PFE/SKT scores plus time-in-service.
promotion_speed.cutting_score_note: WAPS combines test scores, decorations, time-in-grade, time-in-service, and EPR ratings. AFSC-specific cutoffs published annually.

sources.name: AFECD — Air Force Enlisted Classification Directory (DRAFT reference)

notes: DRAFT — verify current AFSC structure, shredouts, and tech school length.

description_official: DRAFT — verify. TACP airmen are Air Force ground warfare specialists who embed with Army and Marine units to coordinate close air support.
description_plain: TACP. Air Force ground warfare. You embed with Army or Marine infantry units and call in close air support when they're in a fight. High-attrition pipeline, infantry-adjacent physical standards, and a tight community. Often conflated with Combat Controllers (different AFSC, even harder pipeline) — TACP is the most common ground-embed AFSC.
description_tldr: Air Force Tactical Air Control Party — embeds with ground units to call in and coordinate close air support.

physical_requirements.details: Passes TACP selection including PAST (Physical Ability and Stamina Test).

training_pipeline.description: Follow-on qualification for many TACP billets.

special_pay.conditions: When deployed to designated combat zones

notes: DRAFT — verify TACP pipeline and JTAC qualification currency.
